final_text:
The bug is clear: in `to_python`, the `ValidationError` is raised without passing a `params` dict containing the invalid `value`, so the `%(value)s` placeholder is never substituted.

{"file":"django/forms/models.py","class":"ModelChoiceField","method":"to_python","parent_class":"ChoiceField","fix_summary":"The raise of ValidationError needs to include a params dict (e.g., params={'value': value}) so that the %(value)s placeholder in the invalid_choice error message is substituted with the actual submitted value."}

parsed_answer.fix_summary: The raise of ValidationError needs to include a params dict (e.g., params={'value': value}) so that the %(value)s placeholder in the invalid_choice error message is substituted with the actual submitted value.
